data.title: Ollie's Bargain Outlet 10-K: $2.65B Revenue, $3.89 Diluted EPS
data.description:
Ollie's Bargain Outlet reported fiscal-year net sales of $2,649.2 million and diluted earnings of $3.89 per share, reflecting growth driven by new store openings and comparable-store sales gains; net income rose to $240.6 million as margins improved. Revenue increased 16.6% year-over-year as the company expanded its store base and leveraged higher merchandise margins despite rising supply chain costs. The results reflect operating leverage from expansion alongside continued opportunistic buying and investments in distribution capacity.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $2,649.2 million, up 16.6% vs prior year.
  • Gross profit: $1,072.9 million, up 17.3%; gross margin 40.5% (up 20 bps).
  • Operating income: $297.7 million, up from $249.5 million.
  • Net income: $240.6 million, up 20.4% vs prior year.
  • Net income per share: Diluted $3.89 (Basic $3.92).

Business Highlights

  • Store expansion: Opened 86 new stores (63 acquired through bankruptcies), bringing the total to 645 stores across 34 states as part of a contiguous growth strategy.
  • Comparable-store sales: Comp store sales growth of 3.7%, contributing to overall revenue growth.
  • Merchandise strategy: Continued opportunistic closeout buying model focused on brand-name merchandise offered at significant discounts (up to 70% off).
  • Supply chain and operations: Gross margin improved despite higher supply chain costs and tariff impacts; company is investing in distribution with plans for two distribution center expansions in 2026.
  • Capital expenditures and investments: Capital spending of approximately $102 million in the year; plans include roughly 75 new stores and expanded distribution capacity, with higher pre-opening and dark rent costs associated with growth.

Original SEC Filing: Ollie's Bargain Outlet Holdings, Inc. [ OLLI ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Eton Pharmaceuticals 2025 10-K: $79.95M Revenue, $(0.17) EPS
data.description:
Eton Pharmaceuticals reported total net revenues of $79.95 million for fiscal 2025, up from $39.01 million a year earlier, driven by higher product sales and licensing. The company posted a net loss of $4.601 million, or $(0.17) per share, compared with a $(0.15) per-share loss in 2024, with results affected by increased interest and amortization expense. Operating loss narrowed to $(0.84) million as higher gross profit was offset by increased G&A and R&D spending.

Financial Highlights

  • Total net revenues: $79.95 million (2025), up from $39.01 million (2024).
  • Gross profit: $42.74 million, representing 53.5% of net revenues in 2025 (down from 60.0% in 2024) due to higher commission/step-up costs.
  • Loss from operations: $(0.84) million (improved from $(2.60) million in 2024).
  • Net loss: $(4.601) million (2025), compared to $(3.823) million in 2024.
  • Net loss per common share (basic and diluted): $(0.17), compared to $(0.15) in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth driven by higher sales of INCRELEX®, ALKINDI and GALZIN and licensing arrangements, including INCRELEX® out-licensing and royalty streams.
  • Commercial/channel shifts: Increased sales volume through pharmacy distributors and third-party logistics providers; company recognized as principal in wholesaler sales.
  • Product portfolio: Eight commercial rare-disease products contributing to brand momentum.
  • R&D and pipeline progress: Increased R&D spending related to ET-600 NDA filing fee and advancement of ET-700 and ET-800; company reports five late-stage candidates.
  • Operational milestones: Completed inventory step-up related to the INCRELEX® business combination, divested DS-200, and recognized licensing milestone payments.

Original SEC Filing: Eton Pharmaceuticals, Inc. [ ETON ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Alumis 2025 10-K: $24.05M revenue, $(2.86) EPS (loss)
data.description:
Alumis reported $24.05 million in revenue for 2025, driven primarily by its collaboration with Kaken that included a $17.39 million license payment and $6.66 million in collaboration revenue; the company posted a net loss of $243.33 million, or $(2.86) per share, with a loss from operations of $(453.80) million. The results reflect a substantial increase in R&D and G&A spending as the company advances late‑stage programs and integrates recent M&A activity. Cash-raising actions during the year extended the company’s runway into early 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $24.05 million, recognized in 2025 largely from the Kaken collaboration (license $17.39M; collaboration $6.66M).
  • Operating income: Loss from operations of $(453.80) million; operating expenses totaled $477.85 million (R&D $385.998M; G&A $91.856M).
  • Net income: Net loss of $(243.33) million for 2025 (includes an income tax benefit of $8.56 million).
  • Net income per share: Net loss per share attributable to common stockholders, basic and diluted: $(2.86).

Business Highlights

  • Clinical progress: Envu completed enrollment in Phase 3 ONWARD1/2 with positive topline results; Phase 2 systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) topline data expected in Q3 2026.
  • Regulatory and commercial preparation: Planning for an NDA submission for envu in psoriasis in H2 2026 with ongoing CMC and filing activities to support potential commercialization.
  • Strategic M&A and pipeline expansion: Closed the ACELYRIN merger in May 2025, acquiring lonigutamab and expanding CNS and peripheral programs, including asset A-005.
  • Partnerships and revenue initiatives: Entered a collaboration with Kaken for Japan, which included upfront and cost-sharing arrangements, with future milestones and royalties tied to dermatology rights.
  • Operational scaling: Significant ramp-up in R&D and G&A headcount, increased clinical trial spending, and expanded manufacturing partnerships; completed a public offering that extended cash runway into January 2026.

Original SEC Filing: ALUMIS INC. [ ALMS ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Sellas Life Sciences Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Sellas Life Sciences Group Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 5 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -8 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 6 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -9 cents to -5 cents per share.

  • Reported revenue was zero​.

  • Sellas Life Sciences Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 5 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $7.66 million.

  • Sellas Life Sciences Group Inc shares had risen by 31.3% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 7.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the biotechnology & medical research peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Sellas Life Sciences Group Inc is $7.50, about 34% above its last closing price of $4.95

data.title: World Kinect Corporation Declares Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description:
MIAMI--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 19, 2026--

World Kinect Corporation announced today that its board of directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.20 per share, which is payable on April 16, 2026 to shareholders of record on March 30, 2026.

About World Kinect Corporation

Headquartered in Miami, Florida, World Kinect Corporation is a global energy management company offering fulfillment and related services to customers across the aviation, marine, and land transportation sectors. We also supply natural gas along with a complementary suite of sustainability-related products and services.

For more information, visit www.world-kinect.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260319846306/en/

CONTACT: Braulio Medrano, Senior Director FP&A and Investor Relations

investor@worldkinect.com

data.title: Brent Crude Retreats After Touching $119 — WSJ
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

Oil and natural-gas prices remained elevated Thursday after the latest round of attacks on Middle Eastern energy facilities stoked fears of a full-blown energy crisis.

U.S. stocks climbed back from earlier declines, trading down less than 0.5% on the day. Oil and stocks both pared their moves after President Trump said he wouldn't "put troops anywhere" when asked about moving forces toward Iran. Trump added that he told Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu not to attack oil and gas fields in Iran, a day after Israel struck facilities linked to Iran's South Pars gas field.

Government bond yields around the world headed higher, with the latest energy-price jump exacerbating fears about inflation. The 10-year Treasury yield rose to 4.281%. The 2-year Treasury yield, which often rises and falls with expectations for Federal Reserve-set interest rates, moved higher to 3.83%.

Gold tumbled, declining for the sixth time in seven sessions. Stocks in Europe and Asia-regions that rely heavily on oil and gas from the Middle East-fell, with the Stoxx Europe 600 erasing its gains for the year so far.

The European Central Bank and its counterparts in the U.K., Switzerland and Japan followed the Fed in holding interest rates steady Thursday, but policymakers signaled they're prepared to hike rates if the conflict drags on.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Assembly Biosciences 2025 net loss narrows
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. biotech firm's 2025 collaboration revenue more than doubled on Gilead licensing deal

  • Net loss narrowed sharply to $6.1 mln from $40.2 mln a year earlier

  • Company ended 2025 with $248 mln in cash and securities, funding operations into 2028

Outlook

  • Company expects to decide on U.S. cost-profit share for herpesvirus program by mid-2026

  • Assembly Biosciences plans to start Phase 2 study for ABI-6250 in chronic hepatitis delta by end-2026

  • Company projects cash position will fund operations into 2028

Result Drivers

  • GILEAD LICENSING DEAL - Revenue growth driven by Gilead's licensing of helicase-primase inhibitor program, including $35 mln option fee

  • R&D SPENDING - Higher research and development expenses due to increased enrollment in HPI program clinical studies

  • ADMINISTRATIVE COSTS - General and administrative expenses rose due to higher professional fees and increased stock-based compensation
